title: [spec-compliance] rf_health adaptive_sf and EMA implementation differs from 02a-coordinated-capacity.md
description:
Reviewed commits ce62ddffe (meshtastic bridge rf_health integration), b3705f1b0 (rf_health.rs + test using ccp16.json), cda09e846 (forbid(unsafe_code)). 

Issues found:
1. RfHealthMetrics uses aggregate EMA/stats (single rssi/snr/density) but spec §2a.3 requires *per-neighbor* EMA (alpha=1/4) integrated with RPL DIOs. record_rx() called without neighbor context in bridge.rs:184.
2. adaptive_sf() logic uses hardcoded thresholds (DENSITY_CRITICAL=20, DENSITY_HIGH=8 etc.) and returns only {9,10,11,12}; does not fully implement AdaptiveSFSelect pseudocode (uses AssignedSF, Utilization>150/200, Neighbor.EMA_Loss>0.25, can downgrade to SF7).
3. should_rebalance() uses loss>40% but spec table/pseudocode uses loss>0.25. Constants like LOAD_HIGH=0.8*FP match table but pseudocode has different Density>10 trigger.
4. Test only exercises ccp16.json subset; ccp_load_balancing.json, ccp15.json not fully validated against independent oracle.
MUST match test vectors exactly and follow normative pseudocode/table. Code differs from spec.

See spec/02a-coordinated-capacity.md:129-152 and test/vectors/ccp*.json. Priority 1 bug.
